channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: On their way! 4 people on NASA Crew-12 mission launch to International Space Station
item.description: The four people are set to dock with the I.S.S. on Saturday, returning the orbital lab to its full complement of seven. NASA's last mission, Crew-11, ...

item.title: RFK Jr. made promises to get his job as health secretary. He's broken many of them
item.description: In his confirmation hearings, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. told U.S. senators that he would not cut funding for vaccine research or change the nation's offic...

item.title: Who will police Gaza, and how?
item.description: Under President Trump's Gaza ceasefire plan, Arab countries and the European Union are supposed to train a new police force in the Gaza Strip. But U.S...

item.title: Can you medal in quiz? Go for the gold!
item.description: Plus: more Olympics, the Super Bowl and some monks.

item.title: A bipartisan effort to save health subsidies failed. Will ICE reform be different?
item.description: A bipartisan effort in Congress to restrain immigration enforcement tactics is flailing despite a Friday deadline to fund the Department of Homeland S...

item.title: 2/12: CBS Evening News
item.description: The FBI announces new details on masked suspect in Nancy Guthrie case; Border czar Tom Homan says Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota is concluding.

item.title: South Africa to deploy troops to tackle crime gangs
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa says the army will work with the police to fight organised crime and illegal mining.

item.title: FBI releases description of suspect in Nancy Guthrie kidnapping
item.description: The 84-year-old mother of news anchor Savannah Guthrie disappeared from her Arizona home two weeks ago.

item.title: See the Olympics schedule for the 2026 Milano Cortina Games
item.description: This is what you need to know about when the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics start and end, along with key games and events on the schedule.

item.title: Sex workers in Nevada fighting to become first in U.S. to unionize
item.description: Nevada is the only state where people can legally purchase sex, and now sex workers at one of the state's oldest brothels, Sheri's Ranch, are fighting...

item.title: CIA releases new video aiming to recruit Chinese military officers
item.description: The CIA has released a new Mandarin-language recruitment video aimed at Chinese military officers, hoping to persuade those disenchanted with corrupti...

item.title: Fresh crew takes off on flight to International Space Station
item.description: The two-woman, two-man crew is replacing four other station fliers who came home early last month due to a medical issue one was having.

item.title: 'Everyone knows somebody affected' - small Canadian town united in grief after mass shooting
item.description: The shooting in British Columbia has been met with shock and sadness, with residents saying nearly everyone has been impacted in some way.

item.title: Rubio warns Europe of new era in geopolitics before big Munich speech
item.description: The US Secretary of State will address the first major transatlantic meeting since Donald Trump threatened to annex Greenland.

item.title: Trump revokes landmark ruling that greenhouse gases endanger public health
item.description: The White House calls it the largest deregulation in US history, but environmentalists say it will prove costly for Americans.

item.title: Journalist Don Lemon set to be arraigned in Minnesota church protest case
item.description: Former CNN host turned independent journalist Don Lemon is set to be arraigned in federal court in Minnesota on Friday, along with four other defendan...

item.title: Scientific studies calculate climate change as health danger, while Trump calls it a âscamâ
Border czar says Minnesota immigration crackdown is over, after angry protests and 2 fatal shootings
